author: Her

capabilities.description: Run a read-only inspection command inside the current workspace. Commands execute directly without a shell: no pipes, redirection, or wildcard expansion. Path arguments must stay inside the workspace. Use grep/find for searching instead of shell globs.

args.description: Arguments passed directly to the executable, one array element per argument. No shell parsing, so quoting is unnecessary and pipes/redirection do not work.

command.description: Read-only command name from the allowlist.

capabilities.title: Run read-only shell command

capabilities.description: Run a side-effect shell command (network via curl, file operations like cp/mv/mkdir/rm, archives via tar/zip/unzip, open files or URLs) after user approval. Commands execute directly without a shell: no pipes, redirection, or wildcard expansion. rm, chmod, mkdir, and touch may only touch paths inside the workspace.

args.description: Arguments passed directly to the executable, one array element per argument. No shell parsing, so quoting is unnecessary and pipes/redirection do not work.

command.description: Side-effect command name from the allowlist.

capabilities.title: Run shell command with side effects

description: Small curated local shell command set: read-only inspection runs freely inside the workspace, side-effect commands run behind user approval. No shell interpretation, fixed system executable paths.
name: Local Shell
systemPromptAddendum: Local shell commands run without a shell: pass one argument per args element, and never rely on pipes, redirection, or wildcards. Prefer shell.inspect for reading; shell.run needs user approval and rm/chmod/mkdir/touch stay inside the workspace.
